questions.prompt:

The proportion of crude oil imports in Japan’s total import value was 18.3% in fiscal year 2007, while it was 16.6% in the previous fiscal year.
In addition, natural gas, which is also a fossil fuel, accounted for 4.6% in fiscal year 2007, compared with 4.0% in the previous year.
In recent years, due to the rise in prices of both crude oil and natural gas, there has been a transfer of income to overseas countries in the form of increased import payments.
For example, the volume of crude oil imports in fiscal year 2007 was 243.1 million kiloliters, which was almost the same as 243.6 million kiloliters in the previous year.
However, the payment amount increased from 11.4 trillion yen in fiscal year 2006 to 13.7 trillion yen in fiscal year 2007, representing a significant increase when compared with 3.9 trillion yen in fiscal year 1997
The same trend can be observed for natural gas. The import volume increased slightly from 63 million tons in fiscal year 2006 to 68 million tons in fiscal year 2007, while the payment amount increased from 2.7 trillion yen to 3.5 trillion yen.
(Source: Ministry of Finance, Trade Statistics)

(1) Regarding the crude oil imported by Japan, which of the following statements is consistent with the passage?

choices.A: The import payment amount for crude oil in fiscal year 2007 was about five times the payment amount for natural gas imports.
choices.B: From fiscal year 2006 to fiscal year 2007, the import volume increased.
choices.C: In fiscal year 2006, the import payment amount for natural gas was greater than that for crude oil.
choices.D: The import payment amount in fiscal year 2007 increased by nearly 10 trillion yen compared with ten years earlier.
